patterns.name: Oracle Staleness / Stale Price Data
patterns.description: Incorrect data supplied by third party oracles (Chainlink, etc.)
patterns.bypass_possible: Only if manipulation (flash loan, sandwich) — not simple staleness
patterns.lesson: CapyFi: oracle staleness = auto OOS on Immunefi

patterns.name: Admin/Governance-Only Functions
patterns.description: Bugs requiring admin/owner/governance access to trigger
patterns.bypass_possible: Only if non-admin can trigger via proxy/delegation
patterns.lesson: Most Immunefi programs: admin trust assumed

patterns.name: D-Bus / Local Access Authorization Bypass
patterns.description: D-Bus authorization bypasses without privilege escalation to root
patterns.bypass_possible: Only if vertical privilege escalation to root is demonstrated
patterns.lesson: AXIS: D-Bus auth bypass OOS unless root escalation chain

patterns.name: Gas Optimization / Efficiency
patterns.lesson: Never a valid finding on bug bounty platforms

patterns.name: Informational / Best Practice
patterns.description: Missing headers, best practice violations without exploitation path
patterns.bypass_possible: Only if concrete exploit chain demonstrated

patterns.name: Frontend-Only Bugs
patterns.description: Bugs that only affect the frontend/UI without backend impact
patterns.lesson: Immunefi: smart contract bugs only for most programs

patterns.name: Known Issues / Acknowledged Risks
patterns.description: Issues already listed in program's known issues or audit reports
patterns.bypass_possible: Only if NEW exploitation path beyond known scope
patterns.lesson: Kiln: 'already known' → CLOSED. Always check known issues list

patterns.name: Latent Bug / Inactive Code Path
patterns.description: Vulnerability in code path that is not active in production (config disabled)
patterns.bypass_possible: Frame as 'growing risk' if config can be changed, but severity likely Low
patterns.lesson: Kiln DeFi: 5 vaults all offset=0 → 'not in production' → CLOSED

patterns.name: Self-XSS / Self-Inflicted Attacks
patterns.description: XSS that only affects the user who injects the payload

patterns.name: Third-Party Service Issues
patterns.description: Vulnerabilities in third-party services/dependencies not maintained by target
patterns.bypass_possible: Only if target's usage of the dependency creates unique risk
patterns.lesson: Many programs exclude third-party service issues

patterns.name: Theoretical DoS Without PoC
patterns.description: DoS claims without demonstrating actual service disruption
patterns.bypass_possible: Only with actual service disruption proof (measured response time)

patterns.name: Already Patched / Fixed Version
patterns.description: Vulnerability that exists only in older, unsupported versions

immunefi_common_exclusions: Incorrect data supplied by third party oracles
Impacts requiring basic economic and governance attacks
Impacts requiring attacks that the reporter combats themselves
Impacts caused by external/third party exploits
